ternalType":"address","name":"receiver","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"inputTokenIndex","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"lpAmountOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"maxAmountIn","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"minLpOut","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"partialFillAllowed","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"deadline","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"cbData","type":"bytes"}],"name":"swapMint","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amountInUsed","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"lpMinted","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"inFee","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"gammaFilled","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"payable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"symbol","outputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"","type":"string"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"totalSupply","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"to","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"value","type":"uint256"}],"name":"transfer","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"from","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"to","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"value","type":"uint256"}],"name":"transferFrom","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"newOwner","type":"address"}],"name":"transferOwnership","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"The contract stores per-token uint \"_bases\" used to scale token units into the internal Q64.64 representation used by the LMSR library. Cached on-chain uint balances are kept to reduce balanceOf calls. The contract uses ceiling/floor rules described in function comments to bias rounding in favor of the pool (i.e., floor outputs to users, ceil inputs/fees where appropriate).**Read-only reentrancy / oracle safety.** The view functions on this interface (`balances`, `LMSR`, `allProtocolFeesOwed`, etc., and the bases/fees and price helpers in `IPartyInfo`) are **not protected against read-only reentrancy** and **must not be used as a same-transaction price oracle by other contracts**. State-mutating entry points perform external token transfers before all storage writes have settled; an integrator that reads pool state from inside a token callback (ERC777, ERC677, custom hook tokens) or any other mid-transaction callback path will observe inconsistent values. For oracle use, integrators must:   1. Read these values only at the start of their own transactions (not from inside callbacks),      or use a TWAP / multi-block aggregation derived from event logs.   2. Never derive a swap price or LP-token price from this pool's `balanceOf`-style reads      without a manipulation-resistant guard (e.g. a time-weighted average). The pool itself does not act on these reads, so its own funds are not at risk; the hazard is integrator misuse. See `doc/security/checklist.md` \u00a7C.2 for the read-only-reentrancy class.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"events":{"Approval(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted when the allowance of a `spender` for an `owner` is set by a call to {approve}. `value` is the new allowance."},"Mint(address,address,uint256[],uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"amounts":"per-token LP-credited deposit amounts.","gammaFilled":"\u03b3 actually applied after the per-window rate-limit cap (Q64.64).        Zero for initialMint. For full fills, equals `gammaRequested`.","gammaRequested":"\u03b3 implied by the caller's requested LP amount (Q64.64).        Zero for initialMint."}},"OwnershipTransferStarted(address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `transferOwnership` nominates a pending owner."},"ProtocolFeesCollected()":{"details":"After collection, the protocolFee accounting array will be zeroed out."},"SwapMint(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"amountIn":"gross input transferred from payer (includes lpFee and protocolFee).","amountOut":"LP shares minted to receiver.","gammaFilled":"\u03b3 applied after the per-window rate-limit cap (Q64.64).","lpFee":"swap-leg fee retained for LPs.","protocolFee":"swap-leg fee accrued to the protocol."}},"Transfer(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted when `value` tokens are moved from one account (`from`) to another (`to`). Note that `value` may be zero."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"LMSR()":{"details":"The returned `effectiveSigmaQ` field is the page-flipped \u03a3q used as the anchor      for `b = \u03ba\u00b7\u03a3q` in the current block (end-of-previous-block \u03a3q so that in-block      `b` is constant). It is populated on read; the storage-side State leaves the      slot zero."},"allowance(address,address)":{"details":"Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called."},"approve(address,uint256)":{"details":"Sets a `value` amount of tokens as the allowance of `spender` over the caller's tokens. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. IMPORTANT: Beware that changing an allowance with this method brings the risk that someone may use both the old and the new allowance by unfortunate transaction ordering. One possible solution to mitigate this race condition is to first reduce the spender's allowance to 0 and set the desired value afterwards: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/issues/20#issuecomment-263524729 Emits an {Approval} event."},"balanceOf(address)":{"details":"Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`."},"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool)":{"details":"The payout fraction `\u03b1' = \u03b1 \u00b7 min(\u03c3_swap, \u03c3_live) / \u03c3_live` so that same-block      burns immediately after a vig-adding swap return pre-swap pool value rather than      capturing the just-added vig (this is the JIT structural defense; see      `doc/rate-limited-mints.md`). Two edge cases bypass the clamp and pay pure      proportional: a full drain (`lpAmount == totalSupply`) and a killed pool. The      LP-token burn is always at the requested `\u03b1`; only the payout fraction is      clamped. There is no \u03c3_swap gate on burn \u2014 burns are always available.","params":{"deadline":"timestamp after which the transaction will revert. Pass 0 to ignore.","lpAmount":"amount of LP tokens to burn","minAmountsOut":"per-token slippage floor on the payout. Zero in any slot means        \"no floor on that token\". Length must equal NUM_TOKENS. Revert string        `\"slippage control\"`.","payer":"address that provides the LP tokens to burn","receiver":"address that receives the withdrawn tokens","unwrap":"if true and the native token is being withdrawn, it is unwrapped and sent as native currency"}},"burnSwap(address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"details":"The function burns LP tokens (authorization via allowance if needed), sends the single-asset payout and updates LMSR state.","params":{"deadline":"optional deadline","lpAmount":"amount of LP tokens to burn","minAmountOut":"minimum output tokens that must be received net of fees (0 = disabled); reverts if amountOut < minAmountOut","outputTokenIndex":"index of target asset to receive","payer":"who burns LP tokens","receiver":"who receives the single asset"},"returns":{"amountOut":"uint amount of asset outputTokenIndex sent to receiver","outFee":"uint amount of output asset kept by the LP's and protocol as a fee"}},"decimals()":{"details":"Returns the decimals places of the token."},"initialMint(address,uint256)":{"details":"Assumes tokens have already been transferred to the pool prior to calling.      Can only be called when the pool is uninitialized (totalSupply() == 0 or _lmsr.nAssets == 0).","params":{"lpTokens":"The number of LP tokens to issue for this mint. If 0, then the number of tokens returned will equal the LMSR internal q total","receiver":"address that receives the LP tokens"}},"mint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Payer provides all basket tokens. Funding mode is selected by `fundingSelector`:      APPROVAL requires `msg.sender == payer`; PREFUNDING requires `msg.sender == payer`;      PERMIT2 uses `cbData = abi.encode(nonce, sigDeadline, signature)` with a MintWitness;      any other selector invokes a callback on payer once per asset.      PREFUNDING assumes the token transfers into the pool and this call are      bundled atomically in one transaction; a non-atomic prefund can be      front-run and consumed by any caller \u2014 see `Funding.PREFUNDING`.      Rounds follow the pool-favorable conventions documented in helpers (ceil inputs).      Reverts with `\"volatile market\"` if the \u03c3_swap deviation gate trips,      `\"rate limited\"` if the per-window \u03b3 budget is exhausted (or partial fill is      not permitted and the full \u03b3 does not fit), `\"slippage control\"` if either      `maxAmountsIn[j]` or `minLpOut` is violated, or `\"insufficient funds\"` if a      funding pull does not deliver the requested amount.","params":{"cbData":"callback data (empty for APPROVAL/PREFUNDING; Permit2 payload for PERMIT2; passed to callback)","deadline":"timestamp after which the transaction will revert. Pass 0 to ignore.","fundingSelector":"Funding.APPROVAL, Funding.PREFUNDING, Funding.PERMIT2, or callback selector","lpTokenAmount":"target LP amount to mint (upper bound; partial-fill aware)","maxAmountsIn":"per-token upper bound on the deposit pulled from `payer`. Zero        in any slot means \"no cap on that token\". Length must equal NUM_TOKENS.","minLpOut":"minimum LP issued for the mint to succeed (post-partial-fill).","partialFillAllowed":"when false, the call reverts if the per-window rate-limit        cap would force `lpMinted < lpTokenAmount`.","payer":"address that provides the input tokens","receiver":"address that receives the LP tokens"},"returns":{"gammaFilled":"\u03b3 applied after the rate-limit cap, in Q64.64","lpMinted":"the actual LP minted (\u2264 lpTokenAmount; \u2265 minLpOut)"}},"name()":{"details":"Returns the name of the token."},"swap(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,bytes)":{"details":"Transfers exactly `maxAmountIn` from payer; fee is deducted from gross output before delivery.      Non-standard tokens (fee-on-transfer, rebasers) are rejected via balance checks.","params":{"cbData":"callback data for callback-style fundingSelectors","deadline":"timestamp after which the call reverts; pass 0 to ignore","fundingSelector":"USE_APPROVALS: payer pre-approves the pool. USE_PREFUNDING: tokens already                         sent to the pool \u2014 only safe when the transfer and this call are bundled                         atomically in one tx, as a non-atomic prefund can be front-run and consumed                         by any caller (see `Funding.PREFUNDING`). Any other selector: callback                         invoked on payer as `selector(nonce, token, amount, cbData)` \u2014 payer must                         transfer the required amount before returning.","inputTokenIndex":"index of input asset","maxAmountIn":"exact input to transfer (fee is on the output side, not added to input)","minAmountOut":"minimum net output tokens to receive; reverts with \"slippage control\" if not met. Pass 0 to disable.","outputTokenIndex":"index of output asset","payer":"address of the account paying for the swap","receiver":"address that will receive the output tokens","unwrap":"if true, native wrapper output is unwrapped and sent as native currency"},"returns":{"amountIn":" actual input transferred (\u2264 maxAmountIn; may exceed if payer over-delivers)","amountOut":"net output sent to receiver (gross output minus outFee)","outFee":"   fee taken from the gross output"}},"swapMint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"swapMint runs a swap-leg (input \u2192 balanced basket) then a proportional mint, all in      one tx. The \u03c3_swap gate is checked against the POST-swap-leg state \u2014 this is what      closes the \"poison swapMint\" surface where the swap leg lands the pool at exactly      `\u03c4\u00b7\u03c3_swap` deviation (the gate's non-strict `\u2265` catches it).      Reverts mirror `mint`: `\"volatile market\"`, `\"rate limited\"`,      `\"slippage control\"`, `\"insufficient funds\"`.      APPROVAL/PREFUNDING require `msg.sender == payer`; PERMIT2 uses a SwapMintWitness.      PREFUNDING assumes the token transfer into the pool and this call are bundled      atomically in one tx; a non-atomic prefund can be front-run and consumed by any      caller \u2014 see `Funding.PREFUNDING`.","params":{"cbData":"callback data (empty for APPROVAL/PREFUNDING; Permit2 payload for PERMIT2; passed to callback)","deadline":"optional deadline","fundingSelector":"Funding.APPROVAL, Funding.PREFUNDING, Funding.PERMIT2, or callback selector","inputTokenIndex":"index of the input token","lpAmountOut":"target LP shares to mint (partial-fill aware)","maxAmountIn":"maximum uint token input (inclusive of fee). Revert `\"slippage control\"`.","minLpOut":"minimum LP issued; revert `\"slippage control\"` if not met.","partialFillAllowed":"when false, the call reverts (`\"rate limited\"`) if the        rate-limit cap would force `lpMinted < lpAmountOut`.","payer":"who transfers the input token","receiver":"who receives the minted LP tokens"},"returns":{"amountInUsed":"actual input pulled from payer","gammaFilled":"\u03b3 applied after the rate-limit cap (Q64.64)","inFee":"fee taken from the input (swap-leg LP+protocol share)","lpMinted":"actual LP minted (\u2264 lpAmountOut; \u2265 minLpOut)"}},"symbol()":{"details":"Returns the symbol of the token."},"totalSupply()":{"details":"Returns the value of tokens in existence."},"transfer(address,uint256)":{"details":"Moves a `value` amount of tokens from the caller's account to `to`. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. Emits a {Transfer} event."},"transferFrom(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Moves a `value` amount of tokens from `from` to `to` using the allowance mechanism. `value` is then deducted from the caller's allowance. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. Emits a {Transfer} event."}},"title":"PartyPool - LMSR-backed multi-asset pool with LP ERC20 token","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"BurnSwap(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted instead of Burn when a burnSwap is executed."},"GuardianChanged(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the Guardian (the emergency role allowed to call `kill()`) is set,         changed, or revoked (revoke = `current` is the zero address)."},"Mint(address,address,uint256[],uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted by initialMint and mint (the LP-issuance side of swapMint emits the         SwapMint event below, not Mint)."},"ProtocolFeesCollected()":{"notice":"Emitted when protocol fees are collected from this pool."},"SwapMint(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted by swapMint, covering both the swap leg and the LP issuance (no         separate Mint event is emitted on the swapMint path)."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"allProtocolFeesOwed()":{"notice":"Protocol fee ledger accessor. Returns tokens owed (raw uint token units) from this pool as protocol fees         that have not yet been transferred out."},"allTokens()":{"notice":"Returns the list of all token addresses in the pool (copy)."},"balances()":{"notice":"LP deposit amounts of each token. This is slightly different than the pool's total balance, which includes any unclaimed protocol fees. The tvl() plus protocolFeesOwed() equals the total pool balance."},"burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Burn LP tokens and withdraw the (value-clamped) proportional basket to receiver."},"burnSwap(address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool)":{"notice":"Burn LP tokens then swap the redeemed proportional basket into a single asset `outputTokenIndex` and send to receiver."},"collectProtocolFees()":{"notice":"Callable by anyone, sends any owed protocol fees to the protocol fee address."},"guardian()":{"notice":"The Guardian address \u2014 an emergency-only role allowed to call `kill()` in addition to the owner.         Returns the zero address when no guardian is set."},"immutables()":{"notice":"Returns all pool immutables in one call. Consolidated to keep PartyPool under         EIP-170; individual per-field getters were removed."},"initialMint(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Initial mint to set up pool for the first time."},"kill()":{"notice":"If a security problem is found, the vault owner or the Guardian may call this function to permanently disable swap and mint functionality, leaving only burns (withdrawals) working. A2zqAzd"]},"src/IPartyPool.sol":{"keccak256":"0xc83b499724c22f1d8555c305efba02b16e027cffa5c99c0487d2a2252e054843","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://7f53d0c7d6dab1ac590caec7fcbe47938d5c1c38c9d0c7624675f36c841652d6","dweb:/ipfs/QmcNdu8joAF4iLceHSA6prVFdoBryJLbgiVfC9Nowj9k32"]},"src/IPermit2.sol":{"keccak256":"0x06fd721baa37c2174a56cff301d8ca6f671cae49bd942e770d3ec880e8dd4cce","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://1c545d16c075e8d1ca8c27e042c270de34f505661525c079de53bac1cfa39af7","dweb:/ipfs/QmXjQ2schyXW77uziMiH77GENiFrmswxaPUNcDjSSXxc7J"]},"src/LMSRKernel.sol":{"keccak256":"0xbd2f87534e6c4ae46b986e9aba0b86e06662421d32d2b171d6ea4316faf70370","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://031afee975bc8974728768d8969d4b3ab5577cb419e43f20b039106ade0e15aa","dweb:/ipfs/QmQMa9gKNjq3iAs71tUkfrm4yihtTUUsuyUwvZGF5ArXRX"]},"src/NativeWrapper.sol":{"keccak256":"0x3c24a1271530ce97216e376105903115a938a44a2f3968747a708835d532d328","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://2abb6bd8905a21e13ccf344332210d3e9f6e18e6768acbe93eba30dca690d135","dweb:/ipfs/QmUibKxx3sgw3WxDNggkoo9chTP8E7HmHrkLhUBjRcc49D"]}},"version":1}

LMSR().details: The returned `effectiveSigmaQ` field is the page-flipped Σq used as the anchor      for `b = κ·Σq` in the current block (end-of-previous-block Σq so that in-block      `b` is constant). It is populated on read; the storage-side State leaves the      slot zero.

allowance(address,address).details: Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

approve(address,uint256).details: Sets a `value` amount of tokens as the allowance of `spender` over the caller's tokens. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. IMPORTANT: Beware that changing an allowance with this method brings the risk that someone may use both the old and the new allowance by unfortunate transaction ordering. One possible solution to mitigate this race condition is to first reduce the spender's allowance to 0 and set the desired value afterwards: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/issues/20#issuecomment-263524729 Emits an {Approval} event.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`.

burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],u.details: The payout fraction `α' = α · min(σ_swap, σ_live) / σ_live` so that same-block      burns immediately after a vig-adding swap return pre-swap pool value rather than      capturing the just-added vig (this is the JIT structural defense; see      `doc/rate-limited-mints.md`). Two edge cases bypass the clamp and pay pure      proportional: a full drain (`lpAmount == totalSupply`) and a killed pool. The      LP-token burn is always at the requested `α`; only the payout fraction is      clamped. There is no σ_swap gate on burn — burns are always available.

params.deadline: timestamp after which the transaction will revert. Pass 0 to ignore.
params.lpAmount: amount of LP tokens to burn
params.minAmountsOut: per-token slippage floor on the payout. Zero in any slot means        "no floor on that token". Length must equal NUM_TOKENS. Revert string        `"slippage control"`.
params.payer: address that provides the LP tokens to burn
params.receiver: address that receives the withdrawn tokens
params.unwrap: if true and the native token is being withdrawn, it is unwrapped and sent as native currency

burnSwap(address,address,uint256,uint256.details: The function burns LP tokens (authorization via allowance if needed), sends the single-asset payout and updates LMSR state.

params.lpAmount: amount of LP tokens to burn
params.minAmountOut: minimum output tokens that must be received net of fees (0 = disabled); reverts if amountOut < minAmountOut
params.outputTokenIndex: index of target asset to receive

returns.amountOut: uint amount of asset outputTokenIndex sent to receiver
returns.outFee: uint amount of output asset kept by the LP's and protocol as a fee

decimals().details: Returns the decimals places of the token.

initialMint(address,uint256).details: Assumes tokens have already been transferred to the pool prior to calling.      Can only be called when the pool is uninitialized (totalSupply() == 0 or _lmsr.nAssets == 0).

params.lpTokens: The number of LP tokens to issue for this mint. If 0, then the number of tokens returned will equal the LMSR internal q total
params.receiver: address that receives the LP tokens

mint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint.details: Payer provides all basket tokens. Funding mode is selected by `fundingSelector`:      APPROVAL requires `msg.sender == payer`; PREFUNDING requires `msg.sender == payer`;      PERMIT2 uses `cbData = abi.encode(nonce, sigDeadline, signature)` with a MintWitness;      any other selector invokes a callback on payer once per asset.      PREFUNDING assumes the token transfers into the pool and this call are      bundled atomically in one transaction; a non-atomic prefund can be      front-run and consumed by any caller — see `Funding.PREFUNDING`.      Rounds follow the pool-favorable conventions documented in helpers (ceil inputs).      Reverts with `"volatile market"` if the σ_swap deviation gate trips,      `"rate limited"` if the per-window γ budget is exhausted (or partial fill is      not permitted and the full γ does not fit), `"slippage control"` if either      `maxAmountsIn[j]` or `minLpOut` is violated, or `"insufficient funds"` if a      funding pull does not deliver the requested amount.

params.cbData: callback data (empty for APPROVAL/PREFUNDING; Permit2 payload for PERMIT2; passed to callback)
params.deadline: timestamp after which the transaction will revert. Pass 0 to ignore.
params.fundingSelector: Funding.APPROVAL, Funding.PREFUNDING, Funding.PERMIT2, or callback selector
params.lpTokenAmount: target LP amount to mint (upper bound; partial-fill aware)
params.maxAmountsIn: per-token upper bound on the deposit pulled from `payer`. Zero        in any slot means "no cap on that token". Length must equal NUM_TOKENS.
params.minLpOut: minimum LP issued for the mint to succeed (post-partial-fill).
params.partialFillAllowed: when false, the call reverts if the per-window rate-limit        cap would force `lpMinted < lpTokenAmount`.
params.payer: address that provides the input tokens
params.receiver: address that receives the LP tokens

returns.gammaFilled: γ applied after the rate-limit cap, in Q64.64
returns.lpMinted: the actual LP minted (≤ lpTokenAmount; ≥ minLpOut)

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

swap(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint.details: Transfers exactly `maxAmountIn` from payer; fee is deducted from gross output before delivery.      Non-standard tokens (fee-on-transfer, rebasers) are rejected via balance checks.

params.deadline: timestamp after which the call reverts; pass 0 to ignore
params.fundingSelector: USE_APPROVALS: payer pre-approves the pool. USE_PREFUNDING: tokens already                         sent to the pool — only safe when the transfer and this call are bundled                         atomically in one tx, as a non-atomic prefund can be front-run and consumed                         by any caller (see `Funding.PREFUNDING`). Any other selector: callback                         invoked on payer as `selector(nonce, token, amount, cbData)` — payer must                         transfer the required amount before returning.
params.maxAmountIn: exact input to transfer (fee is on the output side, not added to input)
params.minAmountOut: minimum net output tokens to receive; reverts with "slippage control" if not met. Pass 0 to disable.
params.payer: address of the account paying for the swap
params.receiver: address that will receive the output tokens
params.unwrap: if true, native wrapper output is unwrapped and sent as native currency

returns.amountIn: actual input transferred (≤ maxAmountIn; may exceed if payer over-delivers)
returns.amountOut: net output sent to receiver (gross output minus outFee)
returns.outFee: fee taken from the gross output

swapMint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,.details: swapMint runs a swap-leg (input → balanced basket) then a proportional mint, all in      one tx. The σ_swap gate is checked against the POST-swap-leg state — this is what      closes the "poison swapMint" surface where the swap leg lands the pool at exactly      `τ·σ_swap` deviation (the gate's non-strict `≥` catches it).      Reverts mirror `mint`: `"volatile market"`, `"rate limited"`,      `"slippage control"`, `"insufficient funds"`.      APPROVAL/PREFUNDING require `msg.sender == payer`; PERMIT2 uses a SwapMintWitness.      PREFUNDING assumes the token transfer into the pool and this call are bundled      atomically in one tx; a non-atomic prefund can be front-run and consumed by any      caller — see `Funding.PREFUNDING`.

params.cbData: callback data (empty for APPROVAL/PREFUNDING; Permit2 payload for PERMIT2; passed to callback)
params.fundingSelector: Funding.APPROVAL, Funding.PREFUNDING, Funding.PERMIT2, or callback selector
params.lpAmountOut: target LP shares to mint (partial-fill aware)
params.maxAmountIn: maximum uint token input (inclusive of fee). Revert `"slippage control"`.
params.minLpOut: minimum LP issued; revert `"slippage control"` if not met.
params.partialFillAllowed: when false, the call reverts (`"rate limited"`) if the        rate-limit cap would force `lpMinted < lpAmountOut`.
params.receiver: who receives the minted LP tokens

returns.gammaFilled: γ applied after the rate-limit cap (Q64.64)
returns.inFee: fee taken from the input (swap-leg LP+protocol share)

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token.

totalSupply().details: Returns the value of tokens in existence.

transfer(address,uint256).details: Moves a `value` amount of tokens from the caller's account to `to`. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. Emits a {Transfer} event.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: Moves a `value` amount of tokens from `from` to `to` using the allowance mechanism. `value` is then deducted from the caller's allowance. Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded. Emits a {Transfer} event.

allProtocolFeesOwed().notice: Protocol fee ledger accessor. Returns tokens owed (raw uint token units) from this pool as protocol fees         that have not yet been transferred out.

allTokens().notice: Returns the list of all token addresses in the pool (copy).

balances().notice: LP deposit amounts of each token. This is slightly different than the pool's total balance, which includes any unclaimed protocol fees. The tvl() plus protocolFeesOwed() equals the total pool balance.

burn(address,address,uint256,uint256[],u.notice: Burn LP tokens and withdraw the (value-clamped) proportional basket to receiver.

burnSwap(address,address,uint256,uint256.notice: Burn LP tokens then swap the redeemed proportional basket into a single asset `outputTokenIndex` and send to receiver.

collectProtocolFees().notice: Callable by anyone, sends any owed protocol fees to the protocol fee address.

guardian().notice: The Guardian address — an emergency-only role allowed to call `kill()` in addition to the owner.         Returns the zero address when no guardian is set.

immutables().notice: Returns all pool immutables in one call. Consolidated to keep PartyPool under         EIP-170; individual per-field getters were removed.

initialMint(address,uint256).notice: Initial mint to set up pool for the first time.

kill().notice: If a security problem is found, the vault owner or the Guardian may call this function to permanently disable swap and mint functionality, leaving only burns (withdrawals) working. The kill() call cannot be reversed and puts the pool permanently into burn-only mode.

lockedBalanceOf(address).notice: Sum of LP currently locked for `account` across all live mint cohorts.         `balanceOf(account) − lockedBalanceOf(account)` is the maximum LP the         account can transfer or burn right now. View-only; does not write back         the pruned head (entries that have just crossed their unlock block are         correctly excluded from the sum regardless).

mint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint.notice: Proportional mint for an existing pool, subject to the σ_swap gate and the         per-window γ rate limit.

mintState().notice: Returns the σ_swap / γ-accumulator defense state in one call.

nonce().notice: This pool's CREATE2 deployment salt (nonce), used for callback verification.

protocolFeeAddress().notice: Address that will receive collected protocol tokens when collectProtocolFees() is called.

setGuardian(address).notice: Set or revoke (zero address) the Guardian. Owner-only.

swap(address,bytes4,address,uint256,uint.notice: Swap input token → output token. Payer must approve `inputTokenIndex` before calling.

swapMint(address,bytes4,address,uint256,.notice: Single-token mint: deposit a single token, mint LP. Subject to the σ_swap gate         and the per-window γ rate limit.
